title: Damn the Consequences. You Must Try: Nana Aba Anamoah || Masterminds Podcast EP54
description:
Download Achieve By Petra – https://app.theachieveproject.com/PSL0001000
Most people are waiting for the right moment, the right opportunity, the right permission. Nana Aba Anamoah never waited for any of it.
In this episode of the Masterminds Podcast, Richie Mensah sits down with one of Ghana's most respected and fearless media personalities — journalist, broadcaster, and champion of women, Nana Aba Anamoah. From being told she was ugly on her first day on TV, to building her own morning show team, to assembling an all-female election coverage unit that silenced every doubter, Nana Aba's story is a masterclass in audacity, preparation, and power. She breaks down the difference between being confident and being powerful, why relevance is a daily discipline, what Women of Valor is really about, and why the greatest mistake anyone can make is simply not trying.

In this episode, you'll learn:

Why confidence without power only opens doors — but power lets you build the house
The difference between being an influencer and being influential
How Nana Aba prepared for the 2010 World Cup when nobody asked her to
Why relevance is a deliberate, daily practice — not an accident
What Women of Valor is really fighting for beyond the surface of feminism
Why the ego is the greatest downfall of men in leadership
How to build a circle of friends who make you better, not comfortable
Why you must damn the consequences and try anyway

title: Timbuktu: The African University That Shocked the World || Untold Africa
description:
For centuries, the world was told that Africa had no history, no scholarship, no intellectual tradition.

But in the heart of the Sahara, a city called Timbuktu was home to one of the greatest centers of learning in the world.

At its height between the 14th and 16th centuries, Timbuktu housed thousands of scholars and an estimated hundreds of thousands of manuscripts covering astronomy, mathematics, medicine, law, and theology.

This is the story of Sankoré University, the manuscript families who preserved knowledge through invasion, and the intellectual empire that history almost forgot.

Untold Africa is a Masterminds original documentary series exploring the powerful stories history left behind.

Welcome to Untold Africa.

References

• Hunwick, John O. Timbuktu and the Songhay Empire (Brill, 1999)
• Jeppie, Shamil & Diagne, Souleymane Bachir (eds.). The Meanings of Timbuktu (HSRC Press, 2008)
• National Geographic, “Saving Timbuktu’s Ancient Manuscripts”
• The Ahmed Baba Institute of Higher Islamic Studies and Research (Timbuktu)
• UNESCO World Heritage Centre – Timbuktu
• Hall, Bruce S. A History of Race in Muslim West Africa, 1600–1960
• Saad, Elias N. Social History of Timbuktu (Cambridge University Press)

He challenges the myth that passion is enough, unpacks why boys and men are falling behind across Africa, and makes a compelling case for why African unity must start at home before it can scale across the continent. This is a conversation about identity, courage, choices, and what it truly means to build a life of excellence as an African in the world today.

title: The Man Who Dared to Unite Africa - Kwame Nkrumah || Untold Africa
description:
In 1957, Ghana became the first sub-Saharan African nation to gain independence.

But for Kwame Nkrumah, independence was only the beginning.

He dreamed of something far bigger,  a United States of Africa.
One flag. One army. One destiny.

In this episode of Untold Africa, we explore the rise, triumph, vision, and controversial fall of the man who dared to imagine a united continent, and nearly made it happen.

From Nkroful to America.
From prison to presidency.
From independence to exile.

Kwame Nkrumah reshaped African history, and shook the foundations of empire.

Untold Africa is a Masterminds original series uncovering the powerful stories history left behind.

Africa is not the story they told us.
It’s the story we forgot to tell ourselves.

🔎 Sources & References

Kwame Nkrumah, Africa Must Unite (1963)
Kwame Nkrumah, Neo-Colonialism: The Last Stage of Imperialism (1965)
Ama Biney, The Political and Social Thought of Kwame Nkrumah
Jeffrey S. Ahlman, Living with Nkrumahism
Richard Rathbone, Nkrumah and the Chiefs
Ghana National Archives – Independence Records
U.S. Declassified Documents on the 1966 Coup

#UntoldAfrica #Masterminds #KwameNkrumah #PanAfricanism #GhanaHistory #AfricanHistory

title: They Defeated Rome - The Warrior Queens of Nubia || Untold Africa
description:
Over 2,000 years ago, Rome believed it could conquer any kingdom.

It was wrong.

In what is now Sudan, the Kingdom of Kush stood independent, ruled not just by kings, but by queens.

They were called Kandakes.

And they did not bow.

In this episode of Untold Africa, a Masterminds documentary series, we explore the true story of the Warrior Queens of Nubia, African rulers who led armies, built pyramids, advanced iron industries, and forced the Roman Empire into negotiation.

When Rome conquered Egypt in 30 BCE, it looked south toward Kush.

Queen Amanirenas responded with war.

Kushite forces stormed Roman outposts.
They seized territory.
They carried off the bronze head of Emperor Augustus as a symbol of defiance.

For years, Rome fought to subdue Nubia.

It failed.

The final treaty demanded no tribute.
Rome withdrew.
Kush remained free.

But their power was not only military.

The Kandakes ruled as sovereign leaders.
They commissioned temples.
They built the pyramids of Meroë.
They advanced ironworking, architecture, and trade.

Long before the world debated gender equality,
African queens were commanding nations.

This episode of Untold Africa challenges the idea that power in the ancient world belonged only to Rome or Egypt.

The Warrior Queens of Nubia were not myths.

They were strategy.
They were fire.
They were sovereignty.

This is Masterminds — where we uncover the leaders and empires that shaped Africa’s rise.

And remember…

You come from greatness.

📚 References

Strabo, Geographica (Book XVII) — Roman account of Kushite conflict
Pliny the Elder, Natural History
Welsby, Derek A. (1996). The Kingdom of Kush
Edwards, David N. (2004). The Nubian Past
Török, László (1997). The Kingdom of Kush: Handbook of the Napatan-Meroitic Civilization
UNESCO World Heritage Centre – Archaeological Sites of Meroë
Archaeological reports from Naqa and Musawwarat es-Sufra

title: The Richest Man in History Was African : Mansa Musa || Untold Africa
description:
One of the richest individuals in recorded history was not a modern billionaire.

He was an African emperor.

In this episode of Untold Africa, a Masterminds documentary series, we explore the extraordinary true story of Mansa Musa, the 14th-century Emperor of Mali,  the man who controlled nearly half of the world’s gold.

In 1324, his pilgrimage to Mecca shocked the global economy. His generosity in Cairo caused gold prices to collapse for years. European cartographers redrew their maps. Africa was no longer a blank space, it was a center of power.

But Mansa Musa’s legacy was not just wealth.

He transformed gold into knowledge.
He built mosques.
He funded libraries.
He elevated Timbuktu into one of the greatest centers of learning in the medieval world.

This episode of Untold Africa challenges the narrative that Africa’s greatness is recent or borrowed. Long before modern empires, the Mali Empire stood as a global economic and intellectual force.

This is Untold Africa, where we explore the leaders, ideas, and moments that shaped Africa’s rise.

And remember…

You come from greatness.

References:

Levtzion, Nehemia & Hopkins, J.F.P. (2000). Corpus of Early Arabic Sources for West African History.
Ibn Khaldun, Kitab al-‘Ibar
Al-Umari accounts of Mansa Musa’s pilgrimage (14th century Cairo records)
The Catalan Atlas (1375)
Hunwick, John O. (1999). Timbuktu and the Songhay Empire.
National Geographic Historical Profiles – Mansa Musa
UNESCO Archives on Timbuktu Manuscripts

description:
Before success.
Before music.
Before Masterminds.
There was my mother.

In this deeply personal episode of the Masterminds Podcast, Richie Mensah sits down with his mother, the woman who shaped his thinking, discipline, curiosity, and love for learning long before the world ever saw his work.

This is not just a conversation about parenting, it’s a masterclass on how minds are built, how values are transferred, and why the foundation you give a child matters more than talent, money, or opportunity.

From discipline and curiosity to education, character, and independence, this episode reveals the quiet principles that shaped everything Richie has become.

In this episode, you’ll learn:

The parenting principles that shape strong, independent minds
Why curiosity matters more than grades
How discipline and love can coexist
The role of education beyond the classroom
How early values shape lifelong confidence
Lessons every parent, teacher, and leader should hear

title: Integrity in a Corrupt World: Israel Laryea Reveals the Truth | Masterminds Podcast EP30
description:
In a world where shortcuts are normal and standards keep dropping, how do you remain principled?
 How do you keep your name clean when the system is designed to break you?
 And what does it cost to stand for the truth?

In this powerful episode of the Masterminds Podcast, Richie Mensah sits with Israel Laryea, one of Ghana’s most respected journalists, to unpack a lifetime of lessons in integrity, reinvention, courage, and doing the right thing even when it’s unpopular.

Israel opens up about the pressures inside media, the stories that shaped him, the battles he’s fought behind the scenes, and the mindset required to stay relevant and respected in a corrupt and fast-changing world.

In this episode, you’ll learn:

How to stay principled in a compromised environment
Why integrity matters more than opportunity
The truth about Ghana’s media ecosystem
How Israel reinvented himself through every season
Why your name is your most valuable asset
Lessons from 20+ years of journalism
What the future demands from creators and leaders

title: The Truth About Ghana’s New Media: Nkonkonsa Tells It All || Masterminds Podcast EP29
description:
Ghana’s new media landscape is loud, powerful, chaotic, and misunderstood.
In this episode of the Masterminds Podcast, Richie Mensah sits with Nkonkonsa, one of Ghana’s most influential and controversial new-media voices, to break down the truth behind blogging, virality, gossip culture, ethics, and the real pressures of running a digital platform in today’s environment.

From the hits to the scandals, the wins to the lessons, Nkonkonsa opens up like never before about building a brand in a world where everyone has an opinion, attention is currency, and one headline can change everything.

This is the raw, unfiltered conversation Ghana has been waiting for.

In this episode, you’ll learn:

How Ghana’s new media ecosystem actually works
What drives virality, and why negativity spreads fast
The pressure of running a platform with millions watching
How bloggers decide what to post (and what to ignore)
The balance between truth, empathy, and responsibility
How Nkonkonsa reinvented himself through backlash & growth
Why new media now sets the national conversation

title: Stress Is Destroying Your Body: Doctor Explains Why || Masterminds Podcast EP28
description:
Most people think stress is just “tiredness,” but according to Dr. Amankwah, it is quietly damaging your heart, hormones, fertility, immunity, and even your brain.

In this Masterminds episode, Richie Mensah sits with his personal physician, the man he says “keeps me alive”, to break down exactly what stress does inside your body, why it shows up as random sickness, and how your lifestyle may be creating diseases without you knowing.

This is one of the most important health conversations you will ever watch.

In this episode, you’ll learn:

What stress REALLY is (not what people think)
Why stress disguises itself as common sickness
How stress destroys your heart & blood vessels
Why stress spikes your blood sugar like you’re running from danger
How stress shuts down fertility in men & women
The link between stress, anxiety, paranoia & depression
How stress creates chronic pain that feels like real disease
What you must do to protect your health

Chapters
00:00 – Intro
02:46 – How Stress Works Inside Your Body
04:55 – The Symptoms You Think Are Illness (But Are Stress)
06:11 – How Stress Makes Existing Sickness Worse
10:07 – Stress and Your Heart
11:48 – Stress, Hormones & Fertility
14:00 – Stress → Anxiety → Paranoia → Depression
20:55 – When Stress Mimics Real Diseases
30:07 – The Real Cure for Stress: Acceptance

title: The Pain Men Hide, And Why It’s Destroying Us: Kobby Kyei || Masterminds Podcast EP27
description:
Men are breaking on the inside, silently.
In this powerful episode of the Masterminds Podcast, Richie Mensah sits with Kobby Kyei, one of Ghana’s most respected digital storytellers and impact advocates, to uncover the truth about male emotional health, invisible battles, and the society that teaches boys to stay silent.

This is not just a conversation, it’s an intervention.

Kobby opens up about purpose, identity, the struggles men hide, and the weight of being “the strong one” that nobody checks on. Rich, raw, and necessary, this episode will make you rethink how you see men, responsibility, and emotional survival.

In this episode, you’ll learn:

Why men are suffering in silence
How society conditions boys to hide pain
The pressure of being the “strong one”
The emotional cost of responsibility
Why purpose can save your life
The role of storytelling in healing communities
How Kobby Kyei transformed pain into impact

title: You’re Stressed Because You’re Focusing on the Wrong Things || Masterminds Podcast EP26
description:
Most of the stress in your life isn’t coming from your problems, it’s coming from your focus.

In this episode of the Masterminds Podcast, Richie Mensah breaks down the Circle of Influence, a powerful mindset framework that shows why so many people feel overwhelmed, anxious, and stuck… even when they’re working hard.

If you’ve been worrying about things you can’t control, trying to fix situations that are out of your hands, or wasting emotional energy on issues that don’t move your life forward, this episode will reset your entire approach to stress and success.

In this episode, you’ll learn:

Why stress often comes from focusing on things outside your control
How the 3 Circles (Concern, Influence, Control) shape your daily life
How to redirect your energy toward what actually makes you stronger
Why obsession with news, online drama, and other people drains you
How successful people control their peace by controlling their focus
How to shrink your Circle of Concern and expand your Circle of Influence
The mindset shift that instantly reduces overwhelm

Chapters
00:00 – Intro
00:57 – You’re Trying to Fix Things You Can’t Control
01:26 – The 3 Circles Explained
02:47 – Your Real Power: The Circle of Control
03:55 – Stop Wasting Energy on Things That Don’t Matter
05:22 – How Influence Actually Works 
08:40 – The Drama That’s Draining Your Life

title: The Woman Behind Ghana’s Biggest Shows: Afeafa Nfojoh’s Untold Story | | Masterminds Podcast EP25
description:
She’s the force behind some of Ghana’s most iconic productions, from reality shows to live events to the moments that shaped our entertainment culture.
But her real story? Many people have never heard it.

In this episode of the Masterminds Podcast, Richie Mensah sits down with Afeafa Nfojoh, one of Ghana’s most respected television and live-show producers.
From growing up in Obuasi, dreaming beyond her environment, walking into Charterhouse without connections, to becoming the beloved leader creators admire, this is the real story of passion, discipline, and heart.

Afeafa opens up about the unique pressures women face in the media space, why positivity is her greatest weapon, how she leads teams with love not fear, and the mindset it takes to thrive behind the camera.

This is a masterclass in resilience, excellence, leadership, and purpose.

In this episode, you’ll learn:

How Afeafa entered the industry with no connections
The real work behind Ghana’s biggest TV shows and events
Why discipline and positivity are her superpowers
How she leads teams with trust, empathy, and high standards
What it takes to excel as a woman in a tough industry
Why creativity requires heart, humility, and constant learning
How to stay grounded while producing at the highest level

Chapters
0:00 – Introduction
2:22 – Meet Afeafa Nfojoh
8:18 – Growing up, Obuasi roots & discovering a love for events

title: Stop Chasing Happiness. It’s Making You Miserable | | Masterminds Podcast EP24
description:
Everyone is looking for happiness, but the more we chase it, the further it runs. This episode breaks down the Backwards Law: the idea that trying too hard to feel good actually makes you feel worse.

In this solo Masterminds episode, Richie Mensah dives into why so many people feel stuck, restless, and unfulfilled, not because life is bad, but because they’re chasing the wrong things. This is a conversation about acceptance, meaning, value, and building a life that feels good from the inside out.

In this episode, you’ll learn:

Why chasing happiness creates anxiety and emptiness
The Backwards Law and how it applies to your daily life
Why “more” never feels like enough
The trap of chasing money, validation, and pleasure
How acceptance creates peace and clarity
The mindset that leads to real, lasting happiness

Timestamps:
00:00 – Intro
01:12 – Why Wanting Something Too Badly Pushes It Away
06:08 – Acceptance: The Real Path to Peace
09:25 – Why the Pursuit of Happiness Leads to Suffering
12:04 – The Hedonic Treadmill & the Illusion of “More”
18:45 – Value Over Happiness: Focus on Becoming, Not Getting
21:33 – The Peace That Comes When You Let Go
23:55 – Final Thoughts: Happiness Happens When You Stop Chasing It

title: Networking Is NOT What You Think: Peace Hyde Exposes the Truth || Masterminds Podcast EP23
description:
Quick note before we begin:
We apologize for the audio challenges in this episode.
The value in this conversation was too important not to share.
Thank you for your understanding and grace.

Most people think networking is about collecting contacts, but real networking is about relevance, authenticity, and purpose.

In this episode of the Masterminds Podcast, Richie Mensah sits down with Peace Hyde, the global head of digital media & partnerships at Forbes Africa, creator of Netflix’s “Young, Famous & African,” and one of the most influential voices on the continent.

Peace breaks down the real truth behind networking:
why most people do it wrong, why some relationships change your entire life,
and how one ride with a stranger completely altered her destiny.

This conversation is powerful, emotional, and full of practical lessons on becoming the kind of person people want to connect with, support, and elevate.

In this episode, you’ll learn:

Why most people network the wrong way
The difference between relevance and proximity
How to build authentic relationships that elevate your life
Why character is more valuable than contacts
How one moment of kindness changed Peace’s entire journey
The mindset required to attract the right people into your life
What real networking looks like in Africa and globally

title: Winners Build Systems, Losers Make Excuses || Masterminds Podcast EP22
description:
Everyone wants to win, but very few are willing to do what winners actually do.
 Most people don’t fail because they lack talent or opportunity. They fail because they rely on motivation, while winners rely on systems.

In this solo episode of the Masterminds Podcast, Richie Mensah breaks down the brutal truth about success:
Your excuses are destroying your progress, and only systems can save you.

This episode will challenge your mindset, expose the patterns holding you back, and show you exactly how to build a life that moves forward even on the days you don’t feel like it.

In this episode, you’ll learn:

Why winners focus on systems, not emotions
How excuses quietly destroy discipline
The difference between “trying” and “building”
Why motivation is unreliable and must be replaced
How to create systems that make success automatic
The mindset that separates achievers from excuse-makers

00:00 – Intro
01:04 – Motivation is weak
02:05 – Building systems through habits

title: Excellence Is Not an Act, It’s a Habit: The Kojo Soboh Story | Masterminds Podcast EP21
description:
Excellence isn’t luck. It’s built one decision, one detail, one habit at a time.

In this episode of the Masterminds Podcast, Richie Mensah sits with Kojo Soboh, the visionary founder of EMI Africa, to unpack the mindset and systems behind one of Africa’s leading event and lifestyle brands.

From his early days in music to leading premium productions across the continent, Kojo shares what it really takes to build with excellence, lead with integrity, and represent Africa with pride.

In this episode, you’ll learn:

What it truly means to live with excellence
How small habits build world-class results
The truth about leadership, branding, and execution
How to stay grounded while building something great
The mindset that separates mediocrity from mastery
What it takes to put Africa on the global stage

title: The Boring Secret Behind Every Successful Person | Masterminds Podcast EP20
description:
Everyone wants to be successful, but very few people are willing to do the boring work that actually creates success.

In this solo episode of the Masterminds Podcast, Richie Mensah breaks down the one principle that separates dreamers from doers: consistency.

No hype, no shortcuts, just the truth about what it really takes to build momentum, stay disciplined, and show up even when it’s not exciting.
 This episode will challenge how you think about motivation, progress, and the process of becoming great.

In this episode, you’ll learn:

Why consistency matters more than motivation
The psychology behind discipline and momentum
How to stay focused when results are slow
The compound effect of small, daily actions
The truth about patience, boredom, and mastery
How to build habits that create unstoppable growth

title: How Losing It All Made Me Unstoppable: The Headless YouTuber Story | Masterminds Podcast EP19
description:
Before he became one of Ghana’s most unique and consistent creators, Headless YouTuber lost everything, his music career, his direction, and his confidence.

But instead of quitting, he reinvented himself.

In this episode of the Masterminds Podcast, Richie Mensah sits with Headless YouTuber, one of Ghana’s most creative storytellers, to unpack the journey of starting over after failure, building purpose through content, and finding peace in anonymity.

This is a story of loss, discipline, and evolution — proof that sometimes, failure is the only path to finding who you really are.

In this episode, you’ll learn:

How losing everything can become your biggest advantage
The mindset required to reinvent yourself
What failure really teaches successful people
Why consistency matters more than motivation
How to build a personal brand around creativity, not ego
Lessons from a faceless creator who built community and impact

In this solo episode of the Masterminds Podcast, Richie Mensah breaks down one of the most life-changing books ever written: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People by Stephen Covey, and shows how these principles can transform your mindset, your discipline, and your entire life.

title: Everything You Know About Money Is Wrong: Obeng Darko - Masterminds Podcast EP17
description:
Most people are not broke because they’re lazy, they’re broke because they were taught wrong.

In this explosive episode of the Masterminds Podcast, Richie Mensah sits with Obeng Darko, a financial educator and author redefining how Africans understand money, value, and power. Together, they break down the myths that keep people trapped in survival mode, and reveal how mindset, systems, and history shape your financial reality.

This is more than a money conversation. It’s a mental reset.

In this episode, you’ll learn:

The biggest lies we were taught about money and wealth
How African education systems condition poverty mindsets
Why “working hard” isn’t the same as “building wealth”
The real meaning of financial freedom
How to use knowledge, discipline, and structure to build lasting wealth
Why financial ignorance is Africa’s most expensive problem

title: The Heartbreak That Created Sisqó - Masterminds Podcast EP16
description:
Before The Thong Song, before the platinum albums, and before the fame, there was pain, heartbreak, and a dream that refused to die.

In this powerful episode of the Masterminds Podcast, Richie Mensah sits down with Sisqó, the legendary artist, songwriter, and front man of Dru Hill, to unpack the untold story behind his journey, from Baltimore to global stardom, and how personal heartbreak became the fuel for his biggest hits.

This is more than nostalgia. It’s a story about discipline, faith, and the price of excellence, the kind of story every creative, entrepreneur, and dreamer needs to hear.

In this episode, you’ll learn:

How heartbreak and struggle shaped Sisqó’s artistry
The real story behind Dru Hill and The Thong Song
How to stay relevant and creative in a changing world
What discipline really means in the music business
The mindset that turns talent into legacy

title: Doctor Reveals the Everyday Habits That Silently Destroy Your Health - Masterminds Podcast EP15
description:
Most people are not unhealthy because of one big mistake. It’s the small daily habits that slowly destroy their health, focus, and energy.

In this episode of the Masterminds Podcast, Richie Mensah sits down with Dr. Vanessa Attikpui, a medical doctor and wellness advocate, to uncover the truth about modern lifestyle habits — and how they’re quietly ruining your body and your mind.

From poor sleep and stress to overworking, emotional eating, and the obsession with “hustle culture,” Dr. Vanessa breaks down how to rebuild your health from the inside out, with science, discipline, and balance.

In this episode, you’ll learn:

The habits that silently destroy your body and brain
The link between stress, sleep, and success
How to take charge of your health as a young professional
The truth about “balance” — and how to create it
How to eat, rest, and move with purpose
Why discipline is the real medicine for a better life

title: 5 Investments That Actually Work in Africa - Masterminds Podcast EP14
description:
Everyone says “invest”, but how do you actually do it in Africa, safely and smartly?

In this solo Masterminds episode, Richie breaks down five proven investment paths that work on the continent: from investing in yourself and using Treasury bills to index funds, two kinds of businesses, and when (and how) to touch speculative bets. No hype. No shortcuts. Just a clear playbook you can start using today.

What you’ll learn

The #1 investment with the highest ROI (and it’s not stocks)
How Treasury bills protect your money against inflation
Why index funds beat “hot stock picks” for most people
The difference between Tap-and-Go businesses vs Skilled/Purpose businesses
When to consider speculative plays—and when to avoid them

Chapters

01:00 – Step 2: Treasury Bills (safe, inflation-beating)
01:56 – Step 3: Stocks (start with Index Funds)

title: From Radio Sales to Charterhouse: The Story of Theresa Ayoade - Masterminds Podcast EP13
description:
What does it take to build an empire, not just an event company, but a creative institution that shapes a nation?

In this episode of the Masterminds Podcast, Richie Mensah sits with Theresa Ayoade, CEO and Co-Founder of Charterhouse Productions, the powerhouse behind the Ghana Music Awards, Miss Malaika, and countless other landmark productions.

Theresa shares how she moved from radio sales to building a company that defined Ghana’s creative industry. It’s a story of vision, systems, and resilience, proving that success is built, not given.

In this episode, you’ll learn:

How Theresa Ayoade started from radio sales and built Charterhouse

The birth and evolution of the Ghana Music Awards

Lessons on scaling, leadership, and legacy

What it takes to build a creative empire that lasts decades

The truth about Ghana’s entertainment ecosystem

How women can lead with excellence in male-dominated spaces

title: 7 Steps to Become Rich - Masterminds Podcast EP12
description:
Everyone wants to be rich, but most people have no idea how.

In this solo episode of the Masterminds Podcast, Richie Mensah breaks down the 7 practical steps to becoming rich, not just in money, but in mindset and structure.

Forget the shortcuts and motivational hype. This is the real blueprint for financial freedom.
From designing your rich life to mastering money management and building businesses, Richie shares the same process he’s used for years to build wealth that lasts.

In this episode, you’ll learn:

How to define your version of a rich life
The importance of budgeting and tracking your money
Building your “Peace of Mind Fund” before you invest
How to get out of debt (and stay out)
Creating a 3–6 month emergency fund
Safe investments that grow over time
How to build businesses that make money while you sleep

title: BlacVolta: Why Most Creatives Stay Broke, and How to Fix It - Masterminds Podcast EP11
description:
So many talented people are creating incredible work, but still struggling to make a living from it.
In this episode of the Masterminds Podcast, Richie Mensah sits down with Joey Adjei, the visionary behind BlacVolta, to talk about the real reason most creatives stay broke — and what it takes to break free from the cycle.

This conversation goes beyond art, content, or culture. It’s about systems, strategy, and self-worth — how to build a creative brand that doesn’t just get likes, but lasts.

In this episode, you’ll learn:

Why creativity alone isn’t enough
The business mistakes most creatives make
How to build a community instead of just a following
Turning creative ideas into sustainable income
The power of structure, collaboration, and consistency

If you’re a creative, entrepreneur, or someone trying to build something meaningful, this one will challenge your mindset and reset your approach to work, value, and money.

“Creativity isn’t about hype, it’s about building systems that make you valuable.”
